steps.title: Identify k as the y-intercept
steps.content: In the equation $y = 5x + k$, the term $k$ is the y-intercept. When $x = 0$, the equation becomes $y = 5(0) + k = k$. So finding $k$ means reading where the line crosses the $y$-axis.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: From the graph, the line crosses the $y$-axis at the point $(0, -8)$. Therefore $k = -8$. A is correct.
